title: The Fix - Global Market Update From Shipfix
section_path: The Fix - Global Market Update From Shipfix
section_path_text: The Fix - Global Market Update From Shipfix
summary:
Main
The Baltic Dry Index surged by nearly sixteen per cent last week on the back of a solid performance for the capesizes. Among the commodities, iron ore was one of the past week's worst performers as Chinese housing data weighed on the demand outlook. By Ulf Bergman

Macro/Geopolitics
Friday saw the release of yet more gloomy data for the beleaguered Chinese property sector.

children.title: Main
children.summary: The Baltic Dry Index surged by nearly sixteen per cent last week on the back of a solid performance for the capesizes. Among the commodities, iron ore was one of the past week's worst performers as Chinese housing data weighed on the demand outlook.

children.title: Macro/Geopolitics
children.summary: Friday saw the release of yet more gloomy data for the beleaguered Chinese property sector. The country's new home prices fell by 0.7 per cent year-on-year in January, an acceleration of the decline from the 0.4 per cent reported for December.

children.title: Commodity Markets
children.summary: After two weeks of gains, crude oil prices declined over the past five trading sessions as traders grew increasingly concerned over the demand outlook, offsetting supply-side issues. The April Brent futures fell by 2.2 per cent over the past week, ending Friday's session at 81.62 dollars per barrel.

children.title: Freight and Bunker Markets
children.summary: The Baltic Dry Index delivered a gain of 15.9 per cent last week, with all segments apart from the panamaxes ending the week in the black. Still, the capesizes provided much of the upward momentum for the headline index.

children.title: The View from the Shipfix Desk
section_path: The View from the Shipfix Desk
children.section_path_text: The View from the Shipfix Desk
children.summary: Copper prices have had a volatile start to the year. While the three-month futures listed on the LME currently are trading broadly in line with the levels seen at the end of last year, the past two months have seen the contracts going through two cycles of decline and recovery.
